name: Session files land in the active project folder
description: Session files go to [PROJECT_DIR] resolved from config, not a fixed dump folder
assertions: Step 2 reflection is written to [PROJECT_DIR]
Step 3 log is written to [PROJECT_DIR]
Step 4 STATE is written to [PROJECT_DIR]
Step 5 NEXT-SESSION-STARTER is written to [PROJECT_DIR]
Step 6 daily note is always written to the fixed Daily folder

name: Session-start reads from the active project folder
description: Start reads STATE and NEXT-SESSION-STARTER from the resolved project folder
assertions: STATE is read from [PROJECT_DIR]/STATE.md
NEXT-SESSION-STARTER is read from [PROJECT_DIR]/NEXT-SESSION-STARTER.md
Daily notes are read from the fixed Daily folder

name: Every close step runs
description: Close never skips a step, whatever the vault state
assertions: The final response shows the full step checklist
No step is skipped citing a missing folder/vault or 'already done'
Step 0 (resolve project folder) runs before Step 1

name: Frontmatter on every file
description: Every file written to the vault carries correct YAML frontmatter
assertions: Reflection has date, type, session_type, tags
Log has date, type, tags
STATE has type, updated
NEXT-SESSION-STARTER has date, type, focus, tags
Daily note has type, date, tags

name: No hardcoded personal folders
description: The plugin resolves folders from config; it hardcodes no user-specific destinations
assertions: session-close resolves [PROJECT_DIR] from config, not a hardcoded path
session-start resolves [PROJECT_DIR] from config, not a hardcoded path
shared-context explains config-driven, context-aware placement

name: Idempotency — steps run even if a file was written earlier
description: A file touched earlier in the session is still executed fresh in the close pass
assertions: Close explicitly states 'already written earlier' is NOT a valid skip reason
Every close step must execute/verify fresh content even if the file was touched earlier
The checklist does not invite ticking [x] without producing the artifact in the close pass

name: Session-track - buffer discipline
description: Track writes no files; items held in a session buffer until close
assertions: Track does NOT write files; items are held in a session buffer until close
Each tracked item captures what / result / decisions / problems
The confirmation response is short and states the running item count

name: Session-handoff - safe, placed, bounded
description: Handoff redacts sensitive info, is placed correctly, and is bounded
assertions: Sensitive info (keys, passwords, tokens, PII) is redacted before writing
The handoff is written to [PROJECT_DIR]/handoffs/ with correct frontmatter (date, type: handoff, focus, tags)
A length bound (under 200 lines) and 'link, do not duplicate' are stated
Exactly one handoff per invocation; wiki-links are flat [[name]]
